phase3_findings.description: marker-less human COMMENTED review on HEAD masks a real APPROVED (false-block wedge)
DEVFLOW_JQ guard checks sourceability not assignment outcome (set -u abort on truncated sibling)
reviews-API-failure test comment claims a non-vacuity mechanism that is factually wrong (guard-invariant verdict)

comment-arm both-markers precedence + cross-arm precedence + ENGINE_ERROR default untested
AC11 exemption is standing, not scoped to this PR — future runs can edit the two workflows freely
SKILL.md:1002 '(both scoped to the current HEAD SHA / run)' parenthetical ambiguous
'final result' wording in PEE test descriptions
truncated-sibling test inherits exported DEVFLOW_JQ -> false RED on correct code
line-start marker anywhere in COMMENTED body classifies verdict-bearing (human quote at column 0)

fix_decisions.evidence: flagged shape 3 (single-source final-pass) but re-affirmed: reproduced by the reviewer ([APPROVED, plain-COMMENTED] -> incomplete); a realistic human action creates a false-block wedge in exactly this issue's class; mutation-checked RED
shapes 2+3 (single-source SFH; fail direction closed via workflow ${VERDICT:-incomplete}; impact is a misdirecting bash abort vs breadcrumb); fixed as trivial symmetry with the gh guard + tests
the narrowing is the issue's authorized deliverable; re-tightening post-merge is follow-up work outside this PR's scope (same shape as #262)

Suggestion-level wording clarification in a SKILL.md descriptive paragraph; SKILL.md edits require the writing-skills RED/GREEN discipline — disproportionate for a parenthetical; substance is accurate one sentence later

fix_decisions.claim_text: anchor COMMENTED marker to first line only
fix_decisions.evidence: the (?:^|\n) line-start semantics deliberately match the downstream grep -E '^## Verdict:' per-line extraction — anchoring classification tighter than extraction would reintroduce the masking asymmetry; the human-quote-at-column-0 exposure equally exists in the pre-existing comment-fallback arm and requires a verbatim unquoted paste
